title: vicia cracca subsp. atroviolacea
intro: Vicia cracca subsp. atroviolacea, commonly known as black vetch, is a subspecies of the Vicia cracca plant, a legume native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems. The health benefits of Vicia cracca subsp. atroviolacea include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and it can also be used as a topical application for skin conditions.
